q: Electrical energy can be transformed into a wide variety of other useful forms of energy.
The transformation of electrical energy into heat energy is very useful and important.
A battery provides electrical power.
The main function of the source of the electromotive force is to send positive charges from the positive terminal to the negative terminal within the source.
When an emf source moves the charges, the source has to do the work.
The electromotive force is actually a force.
A source of electromotive force has an internal resistance.
The internal resistance of a battery depends on its size.
The internal resistance of a battery does not depend on the chemicals used in it.
The internal resistance of a battery does not depend on its lifetime.
The internal resistance of a battery does not depend on its temperature.
The internal resistance must be regarded as being connected in parallel to the battery.
Work has to be done in moving a unit positive charge from one end to another of a resistor.
Work has to be done in moving a unit positive charge through the internal resistor.
The two ends of a resistor must be connected to a voltage source.
When a battery having an internal resistance is used in an electric circuit, the potential difference between the terminals of the battery is always less than its emf.
The emf is an open-circuit voltage.
The terminal voltage is a closed-circuit voltage.
Charging a battery means supplying electrical energy to the battery.
The negative charges are sent from the negative to the positive terminal within the battery.
When two or more electromotive sources (batteries) are connected in series, the resultant emf is the vector sum of the individual emfs.
If the batteries are in series aiding, the resultant emf is the same as the emf of the individual emfs.
If the batteries are in series aiding, the resultant internal resistance is the sum of the individual internal resistances.
If the batteries are in series aiding, the resultant emf is the sum of the individual emfs.
The emfs of the batteries in series opposing are in opposition.
When two batteries are connected in series opposing, the resultant emf is the difference between the individual emfs.
The same polarity terminals of the batteries are connected together in parallel.
In parallel connection of batteries, the same polarity terminals should be connected.
The batteries are the sources of emf.
The generators are the sources of emf.
The dynamos are the sources of emf.
The photovoltaic cells are the sources of emf.
Electrical energy can be stored in other forms for future applications.
The fuel cells can be used to store electrical energy for future applications.
The batteries can be used to store electrical energy for future applications.
The capacitors can be used to store electrical energy for future applications.
The inductors can be used to store electrical energy for future applications.
If the current flows from A to B, the potential at point A is higher than that at point B.
The work done in bringing a unit positive charge from A to B is the potential difference between A and B.
The work done W in bringing the amount of charge Q through a potential difference V is W=QV.
The amount of charge Q passes through a cross-sectional area of the resistor R in the time is Q=It.
When a current I passes through a potential difference V for a time t, the work done is W=VIt.
The work done by the battery in taking the charge through a resistor is the electrical energy supplied by the battery.
When a current passes through a resistor, the electrical energy is transformed into heat energy in the resistor.
When a current passes through a resistor, the atoms in the resistor acquire additional energy.
When a current passes through a resistor, heat energy is produced in the resistor.
An electric motor transforms mechanical energy into electrical energy.
When an electric current passes through a conductor, the electrons in the conductor are accelerated due to the electric field.
When an electric current passes through a conductor, the electrons in the conductor are accelerated due to the voltage across it.
The voltage across a conductor sets up an electric field across it.
The electric field across the conductor exerts a force on the electrons in it.
The flow of current is actually a flow of electrons.
When a current passes through a conductor, the electrons collide with the other atoms in it.
When a current passes through a conductor, heat energy is produced.
When a resistor is connected to a battery, the electrical energy supplied by the battery is transformed into heat energy in the resistor.
The ratio of the electrical energy supplied to the heat energy produced in a resistor is equal to Joule's mechanical equivalent of heat.
If two unequal resistors in series are connected to a battery, the higher resistance one produces more heat.
If two unequal resistors in parallel are connected to a battery, the higher resistance one produces more heat.
Electrical energy can be transformed into a wide variety of other useful forms of energy.
Electrical energy can be transformed into light energy.
Electrical energy can be transformed into heat energy.
Electrical energy can be transformed into mechanical energy.
Electrical energy can be transformed into chemical energy.
Electrical energy can be transformed into sound energy.
Most of the electrical energy supplied to a resistor is converted into light.
The heating effect of electric current has special applications in domestic appliances.
An electric light bulb is made of a glass bulb.
The tungsten has a low melting point.
The electric light bulb produces light energy only.
Electric light bulbs are of various sizes but have the same electrical power.
